content: I've been getting some strange behavior with rust-analyzer as of late, and one of the things I've started to hit - and unfortunately I don't know if this is new or not - is that rust-analyzer doesn't understand the return type of any container_of!() calls. It seems like it just marks all of them as {unknown}, has anyone run into this issue before?

I definitely see some erratic rust-analyzer behavior sometimes (pin_init! was another one, but maybe that has been fixed since). I don't understand much about it so I just wait and hope that one of the in-flight patches will fix them :sweat_smile:
Cc @Tamir Duberstein @Jesung Yang
(I just created a rust-analyzer channel in case it is useful for threads like this)
@Lyude could you tell me more about your environment? kconfig and rust-analyzer version would be helpful for a start

I'm away from my machine so I can't test it right now, but likely due to inherent method calls on the primitive pointer type (e.g., byte_sub, cast) [1]. This should be fixed by [2], assuming rust-analyzer>=v0.3.2727 (2025-12-22) is used.

[1] https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/blob/254f49634ee16a731174d2ae34bc50bd5f45e731/rust/kernel/lib.rs#L258
[2] https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20260507-ra-fix-primitive-v7-0-cb8e3791f2b5@gmail.com/

Tamir Duberstein said:

Lyude could you tell me more about your environment? kconfig and rust-analyzer version would be helpful for a start

rust-analyzer 1.95.0 is my rust-analyzer version.
.config my config is here
There's a couple of weird things on this branch. For one: I can't open up a pair of braces that isn't closed in rust/kernel/drm/gem/shmem.rs without rust-analyzer panicking:
